description: Apollo 12 Flight Journal - Day 6, part 1: Yankee Clipper Rev 24 to 28

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 130 hours, 35 minutes.
The news conference is ready to begin and we'll take down the release line during the conference.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 130 hours, 57 minutes.
There was very little conversation during the News Conference...
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: [131:00:xx] Dick Gordon has 30 minutes remaining in his sleep period.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: [131:21:xx] We have acquisition of Yankee Clipper now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: On the Yankee Clipper, reveille's been held, we've swept down fore and aft, the batteries are all charged, the crew is ready for work, and Yankee Clipper's reporting for duty, sir.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Your two friends are moving right ahead; they're just about ready to egress.
They're running about 1½ hour ahead of time.
They're pretty anxious to get out.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Dick, have a question for you here.
Did you turn the optics power off before sleep?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Roger, copy; you did turn it off?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And I turned it back on and woke up.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And, Dick, when you cycle the H2, O2fans, would you cycle those for 3 minutes?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: That has been done, sir; 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And, Dick, we have a lift-off block data for Rev 25 through 28 when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Lift-off block data read up to Intrepid at 129:31:56.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Dick, you were quite broken up on that; would you repeat that readback please?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Let's go back to Rev 26 for T-14, that is 134:09:59.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Clipper, we have a consumables update when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, first, Dick, if you would, for an uplink, would you go to P00 and Accept.
We'll be giving you a CSM state vector, LM LOS, and updating CMC clock.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And on the consumables update: at 131 plus 00, RCS total, 57.5, 61.0, 55.8, 57.8, 55.3.
And your Delta's from the Flight Plan: first, for the RCS total: minus 8.5 percent, Alpha through Delta: minus 6.8, minus 8.9, minus 8.7, minus 9.6.
And we show that you're 103 pounds below the Flight Plan.
To balance the cryos, would you take the H2tank 2 heater to the Off and O2tank 1 heater, Off?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, H2tank 2 heater's Off, and O2tank 1 heater.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And Pete and Al are just ready to egress; they just depressed the cabin.
If you like, we'll be going over to MSFN relay.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Yes, I'd like to listen to them.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Yankee Clipper, the CMC is yours.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Separate, Simultaneous Comm Link Between Houston and Yankee Clipper in use.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Clipper, would you give us the configuration of your high-gain system?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It's in Reacq now - Servo Electronics, and Secondary.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, we copy that you have - in the Reacq position and say your second comment?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It's Reacq, Narrow Beam width, Servo Electronics in Secondary.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, we have a map update for you when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: If you'd like, we'll hold up until we can get you off relay.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You're going to have to if you want me to hear.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Communications between Houston and Intrepid resumes.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We've dropped the relay; we have a map update, Rev 26, when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Separate, Simultaneous Comm Link Between Houston and Yankee Clipper in use.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Clipper, we read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Dick, they're pressing right on with the EVA, and they're about two-thirds of the way towards - through the traverse and just working their way over towards Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Ed, would you like to have my torquing angles from P52?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Negative copy on those torquing angles.
And we have two map updates for you.
And Lansberg A update P22 and your F-158, Rev 27 update when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm ready to copy, and I can read out the P52 angles, if you want them.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Go ahead and read them down.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, R-1 was plus 0.255, R-2 was minus 0.228, and R-3 was plus 0.141; platform was torqued at GET of 132 plus 45 plus 00.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yankee Clipper, you were breaking up.
We'll see if we can get a little better Comm.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: How you hear me now, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Plus 0.255, minus 0.228, plus 0.141; the platform was torqued at 132 plus 45 plus 00.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Copy plus 0.255, minus 0.228, plus 0.141, and torqued at 132:45:00.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: P22, Lansberg A, Rev 26: T-1 134:05:06, 134:10:08, and you're North 3 miles.
And last PAD is F-158, Rev 27.
Stand by...Clipper, going ahead with that update, T-1 135:21:11, [T-2]135:31:11; T-3 135:42:11, 136:04:11.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: P22 PAD for Lansberg A, T-1 is 134:05:06; T-2, 134:10:08; 3 miles North.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, all back - all readback is correct except for T-2, and that's 135:31:11.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I got some work to do.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We'll be getting you back to relay.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Separate, Simultaneous Comm Link Between Houston and Yankee Clipper in use.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: You can stop your pitch at 232.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Do you have your P22 information?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Do you have your P22 information?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yankee Clipper, Omni Delta, Omni Delta.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Yankee Clipper, your two cohorts are back in.
They just finished another 4-hour EVA; accomplished all the objectives and did an excellent job.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, thank you very much [garble] right now.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: On the High Gain, would you give us pitch, minus 30; yaw, 175?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Just want to let you know that 158 is going along real great.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: [Garble] just to hear something time line.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: If you've got Rev 28 update, I'll take that now before we get into the 158 for the next Rev.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We have the S-158 for Rev 28 when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And I put these on T-1 time, 137:28:55, T-2 time is 137:40:55; Canopus, roll 090, pitch 228, yaw 338; T-1 is 137:49:11; Descartes, roll 89, pitch 217, yaw 319; T-1 137:52:42; Fra Mauro, roll 62, pitch 162, yaw 312; T-1 138:03:47.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And we also have map update for Rev 28.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And we also have one extra thing here.
You can observe a transient event.
You should take photos and observe Alphonsus at a GET of 136:00:02.
You can go with the Hasselblad 80-millimeter lens if you want to take photos, and you can go with F:4, 250, at infinity.
Your brightening between the central peak and West wall, 110 nautical miles South of track, use window number 1, and it can be performed in the S-158 attitude.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I copy at 136:00:02, E1 Hasselblad, 80 millimeter, F:4, 1/250th, and it's going to be area 7, window 1, 110 south, and say what it is again, please?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, Dick, that's a brightening between the central peak and West wall.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'll take a look at it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Is that in the crater Theophilus?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: The transient event, is that in the crater Theophilus?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Again, Alphonsus and a brightening between the central peak and west wall.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: What was the visual on that transient event?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I didn't see anything out there.
There is a dark area in between the central peak and the West wall, but I can't tell what it is.
Looked a little bit darker than the rest of the surrounding area.
It's quite a ways away, so - I really can't see much down there.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Houston, how is that charge on battery A coming along?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yankee Clipper, you can terminate the charge on Bat A at 137:00 as in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Let's start on B, I guess, huh?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay [garble] just be on your toes.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: [Garble] want Charlie to go to sleep [garble].
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: [Garble] don't want Charlie to go to sleep?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, it sounds good, but you're slightly garbled.
Maybe you could move the mike a bit away from your mouth.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I said I didn't want Charlie to go to sleep.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Say, Dick, at 140 plus 00, you have a landmark track coming up of 193.
It's your option, you can go ahead and do 193 or landmark tracking on the LM.
Whichever way you want to go, we'll work you up a PAD.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We can work up a PAD either way.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'll get you a PAD for the LM.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Dick, well, we need some words on the status of that hatch window in order to help us interpret the data you're getting back from the 158.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, it's - it's really not too bad.
It's got a few drops on the outside yet from that Lox stuff that's on there and I've been trying to keep the inside clean.
It's got some smear marks on the inside pane where we've tried to wipe it.
It got all wet last night and I tried to dry it off this morning with a towel and some Kleenex.
It's still smeared a little, but other than that, it looks pretty good; really not too bad.
I'll clean it again right now.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Pete and Al are just finishing up the post-EVA, and it looks as though they're pretty far ahead.
They have a little bit of time to sit back end relax.
We have a map update for Rev 29 when you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, I'm - I'm ready to copy.
Tell those guys to sit back and relax.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Tell those guys they deserve a rest.
They can sit back and relax.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Ed, it sounds like they didn't have any problems down there at all.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: They ran into a few things; but, as they did yesterday, they overcame them.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I expect most of their problems are human.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Maybe so, Dick, but not many of them errors.
Dick, will you go back in your Flight Plan to 131:30?
And we missed a crew status report at that point.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Says here I had 7 hours sleep, PRD [garble] 110 [garble].
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We still have a lot of garbling, and broken up.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid from 137:38:34 to 137:51:10.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And you're looking for my status report last night.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We copy 7 hours and 11019; and, Dick, we're having a little bit of trouble understanding.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm wondering if the microphone is in - the correct position relative to your mouth.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: How do you hear me now?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: I'm holding it close enough to my mouth.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, we show a real strong signal down here, but the transmission's still slightly garbled.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'll try to work on it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Dick, would you go ahead and stir the cryos for 3 minutes?
We'd like to see where we stand with respect to balancing.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Houston, are you copying those torquing angles?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, Houston, the cryos have been stirred for 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: It looks as though you're almost balanced.
About one more Rev ought to do it, and then you'll be going back to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'd like to give you the T-17 and T-18 lift-off PAD's before CSM LOS, coming up in about 8 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Yankee Clipper, are you ready to copy.?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Comm continues between Houston and Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: See you on the other side.
transcript.speaker: Gibson
